description.value:
Written by the sorcerer Thoth-Amon of pre-Egyptian Stygia, discussing at length the entity known as The Prolonged of Life, a possible synonym for Charon, the ferryman of the river of Styx. This entity appears as a large, hooded giant draped in cloth and will later be known as Tawil al-'Umr, an avatar of Yog-Sothoth. Includes spells for communing with gods and speaking with animals, as well as more destructive flavors of Mythos magic.

LanguageStygian and ancient Egyptian
AuthorThoth-Amon
Date10,000 B.C.
Sanity Loss1D3/1D8
Cthulhu Mythos+1/+8 percentiles
Occult+5 percentiles
Mythos Rating27
Study (weeks)32

Spells Contained

  • Apportion Ka
  • Beseech Charon (Contact Yog-Sothoth)
  • Chant of Thoth
  • Command Animal
  • Contact Tomb-Herd

system.author: Thoth-Amon
system.spells: Apportion Ka, Beseech Charon (Contact Yog-Sothoth), Chant of Thoth, Command Animal, Contact Tomb-Herd
